steps.title: Use the rectangle area formula
steps.content: For a rectangle, area = length × width. With area 63 square meters and length 9 meters, set up $63 = 9w$ and solve for the width.

steps.title: Apply the rectangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is found using Area = length × width. We know the area is 63 square meters and the length is 9 meters. Substituting these values into the formula: $$63 = 9 \times \text{width}$$

steps.title: Solve for width
steps.content: Divide both sides by 9 to isolate the width: $$\text{width} = \frac{63}{9} = 7$$ The width is 7 meters. That is the correct answer.
